title: Generative Media Phase Complete
concept: PHASE E CAPSTONE. Concludes the Generative Media phase. The boss exercise explain MUST end with the required handoff: 'Phase E: Generative Media is complete. Next up: Large Language Models.'

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of Generative Media models?

options.text: Creating new images, audio, and video
Deleting duplicate files on a hard drive

exercises.explain: Generative Media is all about creating new, original content rather than just analyzing or labeling existing files.

cards.title: Our Generative Journey
cards.body: We have journeyed through the magic of teaching computers how to paint, sing, and create! Let's review how we turned math into breathtaking digital art.

cards.title: What is Generative Media?
cards.body: Generative Media refers to using AI models (like GANs, Diffusion Models, and VAEs) to create brand-new content such as realistic images, synthetic voices, music, and videos from simple text prompts or random math noise.

cards.title: The Digital Master Artist
cards.body: Think of generative media like a master artist who has learned how to paint, compose music, and write stories. Instead of just looking at pictures, you can now ask the artist to paint a completely new sunset over a neon city, and they can create it instantly.

cards.title: Prompt to Picture
cards.body: When you type 'a astronaut riding a horse on Mars' into an image generator, the AI doesn't search the web. It uses its learned understanding of 'astronaut', 'horse', and 'Mars' to draw a unique high-resolution image from scratch.

cards.title: The Copy-Paste Myth
cards.body: Many believe AI simply copy-pastes pieces of existing images from the internet. In reality, the AI learns concepts (like 'fur' or 'sky') and generates completely new pixels that have never existed before.

cards.title: Classifier vs. Creator
cards.body: See how generative models differ from traditional classification models.

right.text: Takes the label 'Cat' and draws a new picture of a cat.

left.text: Looks at a picture of a cat and labels it 'Cat'.

exercises.prompt: What are the two competing neural networks inside a GAN called?

options.text: The Encoder and the Decoder only
The Input Layer and the Output Layer

exercises.explain: Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) consist of two neural networks: the Generator (which creates fake data) and the Discriminator (which tries to catch the fakes).

exercises.prompt: True or False: Generative AI models can only be used to create visual images, not audio or music.
exercises.explain: Generative models are not limited to images; they can also generate music, synthetic voices, text, and 3D shapes.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: The generative model that starts with pure noise and cleans it up step-by-step is called a ___ model.
exercises.sentence: The generative model that starts with pure noise and cleans it up step-by-step is called a Diffusion model.
exercises.explain: Diffusion models work by adding noise to an image and learning how to step-by-step remove it to reveal a clean, new picture.

exercises.prompt: Spot the AI: Which of these images is most likely generated by an AI model?

options.text: Image A: A photo of a woman wearing symmetrical glasses in front of a brick wall.
Image B: A portrait showing asymmetric glasses frames and floating hair strands.
Image C: A hand-drawn sketch of a cat resting on a window sill.

exercises.explain: Image B is generated by AI, as indicated by the classic AI generation glitch of asymmetric glasses and floating hair strands that don't connect to the scalp.

exercises.prompt: Which statement best summarizes what we have learned about Generative Media?

options.text: Generative AI can only copy existing media files pixel for pixel.
Generative AI learns underlying patterns to create entirely new images and sounds.
Generative AI only works with text inputs and cannot handle video or audio.
Generative AI requires humans to manually draw every pixel.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Phase E: Generative Media is complete. Next up: Large Language Models.
